learningGoals: Identify the basic functions of a wetland in filtering city water.
Use comparative adjectives (cleaner, dirtier, clearer) to describe water samples.
Build a natural water filtration model using sand, gravel, and charcoal.
lessons.activitySummary: Students collect water samples from the park's ponds and test their clarity. They then work in teams to build a multi-layer natural filter and record how the water changes as it passes through.
lessons.safetyNotes: Stay 2 meters away from the water edge; use long-handled cups for sampling; wash hands after touching pond water.

lessons.location: East Lake Wetland Park (东湖湿地公园 - 落雁景区)
learningGoals: Describe bird physical traits and behaviors using the present continuous tense.
Explain how different beak shapes help birds eat specific foods.
Use binoculars and a field guide to identify at least 3 local bird species.
lessons.activitySummary: Students go on a guided bird walk to spot migratory birds like the Grey Heron. They complete a 'Beak and Food' matching challenge and sketch birds in their field journals.
lessons.safetyNotes: Keep noise levels low to avoid scaring wildlife; stay on the designated boardwalks; wear warm clothing.

learningGoals: Classify aquatic plants into floating, submerged, and emergent categories.
Use sequence adverbs (first, next, then) to explain the process of photosynthesis.
Measure and record leaf dimensions to practice data collection.
lessons.activitySummary: Students participate in a botanical scavenger hunt to find specific wetland plants. They use rulers to measure plant height and create leaf rubbings to study vein patterns.
lessons.safetyNotes: Wear sunscreen and hats; watch out for slippery stones near the lotus ponds; do not pick or eat any wild plants.

learningGoals: Propose 3 ways to reduce human impact on urban wetlands.
Use modal verbs (should/shouldn't) to create park rules for visitors.
Design a model of a 'Trash Trap' to prevent plastic from entering the lake.
lessons.activitySummary: Students conduct a 'Trash Audit' to see what types of waste are near the lake. They then use recycled materials to design and pitch a prototype device that protects the wetland from city pollution.
lessons.safetyNotes: Use gloves and tongs when picking up trash; avoid sharp objects like broken glass; stay within sight of the instructors.
